stories.title: SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
stories.abstract: Threat actor UTA0533 exploited two zero-day vulnerabilities in SonicWall SMA 1000 series appliances to achieve root-level access. The campaign, active since late June 2026, involved deploying custom malware to compromised VPN gateways before the flaws were publicly disclosed.
stories.why: Attackers gained persistent, high-privilege access to network perimeters, potentially facilitating lateral movement and long-term espionage within affected corporate environments.
stories.action: Update SonicWall SMA 1000 series appliances to the latest patched firmware versions immediately.

stories.title: CISA Adds Three Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
stories.abstract: Broadcom and Fortinet have released critical security updates addressing multiple vulnerabilities across VMware Avi Load Balancer and various Fortinet products. Several of these flaws, including command injection vulnerabilities, are currently being actively exploited in the wild and have been added to CISA's KEV catalog.
stories.why: Active exploitation of these vulnerabilities poses a high risk of unauthorized system access and remote code execution within enterprise environments.
stories.action: Apply the latest security patches for VMware Avi Load Balancer, FortiSandbox, FortiAnalyzer, FortiManager, and FortiDDoS immediately.

stories.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: WordPress RCE, SonicWall 0-Days, AI Service Attacks, SharePoint 0-Day and More
stories.abstract: Recent security disclosures highlight a surge in critical vulnerabilities across enterprise infrastructure, including remote code execution flaws in WordPress and SonicWall devices. Attackers are actively exploiting these gaps, alongside SharePoint weaknesses, to bypass security controls and compromise sensitive system data.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities provide direct entry points for attackers to gain unauthorized access, exfiltrate data, or disable defensive infrastructure within enterprise environments.
stories.action: Audit and patch all internet-facing SonicWall, WordPress, and SharePoint instances immediately.

stories.title: WP2Shell WordPress Vulnerabilities Exploited in the Wild
stories.abstract: Two recently disclosed vulnerabilities in WordPress, identified as CVE-2026-60137 and CVE-2026-63030, are currently being actively exploited by attackers in the wild. Threat actors began targeting these flaws shortly after their public disclosure.
stories.why: Active exploitation of WordPress core vulnerabilities poses a significant risk of site compromise and unauthorized remote code execution.
stories.action: Update WordPress installations to the latest patched version immediately.

stories.title: World's Largest AI Model Repository Hugging Face Breached by Autonomous AI Agent
stories.abstract: Hugging Face reported a security breach involving unauthorized access to its production infrastructure. Attackers compromised internal datasets and service credentials, reportedly utilizing an autonomous AI agent system to facilitate the intrusion.
stories.why: Compromised credentials and datasets on AI platforms pose significant risks to supply chain integrity and proprietary model security.
stories.action: Rotate all access tokens and review account activity immediately.

stories.title: New Orleans Cops Published Policy Document Allowing Weaponized Drones
stories.abstract: New Orleans police department documentation reveals the agency has established formal policies regarding the potential deployment of weaponized unmanned aerial vehicles, signaling a shift toward integrating lethal force capabilities into municipal drone operations.
stories.why: This indicates a significant escalation in domestic law enforcement tactical capabilities and raises critical concerns regarding oversight, civil liberties, and automated force application.

stories.title: Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack
stories.abstract: Ernst & Young confirmed a data breach involving a third-party IT support ticketing platform. Unauthorized actors accessed sensitive records, including Social Security numbers, financial details, and personal contact information belonging to individuals managed within the compromised system.
stories.why: Third-party vendor risk remains a critical attack vector for large enterprises, often bypassing internal security controls to expose sensitive client data.

stories.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
stories.abstract: CISA has added a critical deserialization vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint Server, tracked as CVE-2026-58644, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. This flaw allows for remote code execution and is currently being leveraged in active cyberattacks.
stories.why: The vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and is actively exploited, posing a significant risk of unauthorized system compromise.
stories.action: Apply the latest Microsoft security patches for SharePoint Server immediately.

stories.title: Critical ServiceNow code execution flaw now exploited in attacks
stories.abstract: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical remote code execution vulnerability within the ServiceNow AI platform. The flaw allows unauthorized parties to execute arbitrary commands, posing a significant risk to organizations relying on the service for enterprise workflow management.
stories.why: Successful exploitation grants attackers full control over the affected ServiceNow instance, potentially leading to data exfiltration and lateral movement within corporate networks.
stories.action: Apply the latest security patches provided by ServiceNow immediately.

stories.title: New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
stories.abstract: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in 7-Zip, tracked as CVE-2026-14266, allows attackers to execute arbitrary code by tricking users into opening malicious XZ archive files. The flaw stems from improper handling of chunked data during the extraction process.
stories.why: Arbitrary code execution via common archive utilities poses a significant risk for initial access and malware delivery on end-user systems.

stories.title: HollowByte DDoS flaw bloats OpenSSL server memory with 11-byte payload
stories.abstract: OpenSSL addressed a denial-of-service vulnerability, nicknamed HollowByte, that allows unauthenticated attackers to exhaust server memory. By sending a specific 11-byte payload, an adversary can force the system to perform excessive buffer pre-allocations that remain unreleased, eventually crashing the service.
stories.why: Memory exhaustion vulnerabilities in core cryptographic libraries pose significant availability risks to any infrastructure relying on OpenSSL for secure communications.
stories.action: Update OpenSSL to the latest patched version.

stories.title: Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
stories.abstract: A threat actor identified as bandcampro leveraged Google's Gemini CLI tool to automate botnet management and credential cracking. Analysis of session logs confirms the attacker utilized AI capabilities to maintain control over compromised dental clinic systems.
stories.why: This demonstrates how attackers are integrating generative AI tools into their operational workflows to streamline malicious tasks and scale botnet management.

stories.title: Hugging Face warns an autonomous AI agent hacked its network
stories.abstract: Hugging Face reported a security breach involving its production infrastructure. Attackers utilized an autonomous AI agent to infiltrate the network, successfully compromising internal datasets and sensitive credentials stored within the platform.
stories.why: This incident highlights the emerging risk of AI-driven automated attacks targeting development platforms and the potential for credential exposure in AI supply chains.
stories.action: Rotate all API keys and secrets stored within Hugging Face environments immediately.

stories.title: Hackers stole ‘significant’ amount of data from tech firm relied on by thousands of US hospitals and pharmacies
stories.abstract: Craneware, a software provider for thousands of American healthcare facilities, confirmed a cyberattack resulted in the theft of sensitive customer information. The breach potentially exposes patient billing and health records managed through the company's financial and operational platforms.
stories.why: This incident impacts a critical supply chain node in the US healthcare sector, potentially compromising vast amounts of sensitive protected health information.

stories.title: Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
stories.abstract: F5 released patches for a critical heap buffer overflow vulnerability in NGINX. Unauthenticated remote attackers can exploit this flaw using malicious HTTP requests to crash worker processes, potentially leading to remote code execution or service denial.
stories.why: This vulnerability affects core web infrastructure, allowing attackers to disrupt availability or gain unauthorized control over server processes.
stories.action: Update NGINX to 1.30.4, 1.31.3, or NGINX Plus 37.0.3.1 immediately.

stories.title: Romania races to restore land registry after cyberattack disrupts property market
stories.abstract: Romania's national land registry is currently offline following a severe cyberattack. The agency describes the incident as the most significant technical disruption in its history, effectively halting property market transactions across the country while recovery efforts remain ongoing.
stories.why: National infrastructure outages disrupt critical economic sectors and highlight vulnerabilities in government record-keeping systems.

stories.title: Microsoft 365 calendars become spy drop boxes in HOLLOWGRAPH campaign
stories.abstract: The HollowGraph malware campaign leverages Microsoft 365 calendar appointments set for the year 2050 to store malicious commands. By utilizing Microsoft Graph APIs for command-and-control communication, attackers effectively blend malicious traffic with legitimate cloud activity to evade detection.
stories.why: This technique exploits trusted cloud infrastructure for C2, making traditional network-based detection and traffic filtering significantly more difficult for security teams.

stories.title: Microsoft Exchange Hacked, Five Years Later
stories.abstract: A security researcher is finally disclosing internal details regarding the 2021 HAFNIUM campaign against Microsoft Exchange. The upcoming presentation will reveal how the operation unfolded from within the attackers' infrastructure, contrasting the initial public narrative that the breach was limited in scope.
stories.why: Understanding the operational tradecraft and internal decision-making of state-sponsored actors improves incident response and threat modeling for large-scale enterprise infrastructure.

stories.title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices
stories.abstract: Policymakers are debating whether to prohibit ransom payments as ransomware groups employ more advanced tactics. The discussion highlights the ongoing struggle to balance victim recovery with the need to dismantle the financial incentives driving these criminal operations.
stories.why: Legislative changes regarding ransom payments could fundamentally alter incident response strategies and legal liabilities for organizations targeted by cyber extortionists.

stories.title: JadePuffer Returns With Ransomware Designed to Wipe AI Models
stories.abstract: The threat actor known as JadePuffer has launched a new campaign utilizing the ENCFORGE ransomware. This variant is specifically engineered to target and permanently destroy artificial intelligence model artifacts, posing a unique risk to organizations relying on proprietary machine learning assets.
stories.why: This represents an emerging trend of ransomware targeting specialized data structures, specifically threatening the integrity and availability of AI-driven intellectual property.

stories.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, July 20th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/10014, (Mon, Jul 20th)
stories.abstract: The provided source material for the July 20th, 2026, SANS ISC Stormcast is empty. No specific security incidents, vulnerabilities, or operational updates were detailed in the provided metadata.

stories.title: Scans for Hikvision Intelligent Security API, (Sun, Jul 19th)
stories.abstract: Security researchers report ongoing, widespread internet scanning activity targeting the Intelligent Security API found in Hikvision camera systems. These devices remain a frequent focus for automated exploitation attempts due to their historical susceptibility to various security flaws.
stories.why: Hikvision devices are frequently compromised to build botnets or gain persistent access to internal networks.

stories.title: Hackers abuse ViPNet software to target Russian govt agencies
stories.abstract: Sophisticated threat actors are exploiting the update delivery system of ViPNet, a secure networking suite, to compromise Russian government entities and other organizations. The attackers are leveraging the software's legitimate update process to deploy malicious payloads into targeted environments.
stories.why: Supply chain compromise via trusted update mechanisms represents a high-severity risk for organizations relying on third-party security software.
stories.action: Audit ViPNet update logs and monitor for unauthorized traffic originating from the update service.

stories.title: Watch Flock Safety CEO Garrett Langley discuss the future of surveillance at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
stories.abstract: Flock Safety CEO Garrett Langley is scheduled to discuss the intersection of public safety and individual privacy at the upcoming TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 conference.
stories.why: The company's widespread deployment of automated license plate readers and surveillance technology remains a significant point of contention for privacy advocates and security professionals.

stories.title: New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges
stories.abstract: A researcher identified a zero-day vulnerability, named LegacyHive, that enables local privilege escalation on fully patched Windows systems. The exploit leverages specific registry hive manipulation to grant attackers administrative access, though details remain limited as the researcher disclosed the flaw publicly.
stories.why: This vulnerability allows unprivileged users to gain full administrative control, significantly increasing the impact of initial access during a system compromise.

stories.title: Researchers Build WordPress Exploit Using OpenAI's GPT
stories.abstract: A security researcher successfully leveraged OpenAI's latest large language model to automate the creation of an exploit chain targeting a critical vulnerability within the WordPress ecosystem.
stories.why: This demonstrates how generative AI can lower the barrier to entry for developing functional exploits from vulnerability disclosures.

stories.title: Three Steps to the Terminal: A Siemens ROX II Zero-Day Trilogy
stories.abstract: Researchers identified a chain of three zero-day vulnerabilities affecting Siemens ROX II industrial switches. These flaws allow an attacker to escalate privileges and establish persistent root-level access to the device's operating system.
stories.why: Compromised OT infrastructure can lead to unauthorized network control, process disruption, or lateral movement within critical industrial environments.
stories.action: Contact Siemens support for firmware updates or mitigation guidance regarding ROX II devices.

stories.title: Software provider to more than 2,000 US hospitals says hackers stole employee and customer data
stories.abstract: Craneware, a software provider serving over 2,000 U.S. hospitals, confirmed a data breach involving unauthorized access to its internal systems. The company is currently working with forensic investigators to determine the scope of the compromised employee and customer information.
stories.why: The incident exposes sensitive data within the healthcare supply chain, potentially impacting a vast network of medical institutions and their patient records.

stories.title: The ACLU Is Arming Lawyers to Expose State Surveillance Secrets
stories.abstract: The ACLU has released a legal toolkit for Massachusetts attorneys designed to challenge the use of undisclosed surveillance technologies, such as facial recognition and AI-generated police reports, in criminal proceedings.
stories.why: This initiative increases transparency regarding law enforcement's use of opaque algorithmic tools, potentially setting precedents for challenging digital evidence in court.
